comments.author: amy-why-3459
comments.body:
Overall, I agree with the direction of this PR. Turning “wrong endpoint crashes the engine/stage process” into an explicit, explainable 400 response is the right abstraction. The Qwen3-Omni /v1/completions case is not an engine failure; it is an endpoint/pipeline semantic mismatch and should be rejected before the request enters the pipeline.

A few suggestions:

**1. Endpoint restrictions should be resolved from the final pipeline, not the auto-detected pipeline.**
I agree with the existing review comment here. get_pipeline_config() may return the registry auto-detected pipeline first, but _create_from_registry() can later load deploy_config_path and switch to deploy_cfg.pipeline. If endpoint restrictions are resolved before deploy overrides, we may apply allow/reject policy from a pipeline that is not actually running.

**2. I suggest resolving endpoint restrictions after final pipeline resolution, or adding a regression test that covers deploy YAML pipeline overrides.**

Please audit other Qwen3-Omni endpoints, not only /v1/completions.
From the current vLLM-Omni route registration pattern, many omni-specific endpoints are globally registered and then handled inside ea

comments.author: alex-jw-brooks
comments.body:
Hi @hsliuustc0106 @yuanheng-zhao @amy-why-3459, thanks for the detailed thoughts! I had spent a bit of time testing other endpoints, but I think the completions one is the most severe risk since it fully crashes the server. This is intended to be a general pattern though, and not a Qwen3Omni specific hack. I think some good things to follow up are:

1. Audit other models to make sure they can't be crashed by sending basic requests to an unsupported endpoint.
2. Have a follow up to the [tiny model test pr](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-omni/pull/4524) that runs a valid payload for every endpoint and ensures the server is still alive after.

To help with these, I think we can do something like the script below. It's mostly generated with Claude, so checking with correctness should probably be done, but it runs fast, and running it against `main` does trigger the server crash the for completions. 🙂 
<details>
    <summary>Script for hitting every endpoint</summary>

```python
import argparse
import asyncio
import json
import re

import requests
import websockets

CRASH = 0
PROBED: set[tuple[str, str]] = set()
DUMMY_PNG = (
    b"\x89PNG\r\n\x1a\n"
    b"\x

comments.author: yuanheng-zhao
comments.body:
@alex-jw-brooks I tested with your cmd and script and just left a record here. Please take a look if these are consistent with our expectations. Launch server: ```bash vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct --omni --port 8998 --enforce-eager ``` Run with the script in https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-omni/pull/4762#issuecomment-4850262384
The output of python collect_env.py ```text Server: http://localhost:8998 Model: Qwen/Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct Voice: aiden Timeout: 120s (HTTP), 5s (WS) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── === Info / Health === GET /health 200 0B GET /load 200 17B json GET /version 200 20B json GET /metrics 200 159042B text/plain GET /ping 200 0B POST /ping 200 0B GET /v1/models 200 499B json === T

comments.author: alex-jw-brooks
comments.body:
Thanks @yuanheng-zhao! They unfortunately do surface to the user, but they don't crash the server like the completions one did. I actually fixed the tokenization routing in this PR https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm-omni/pull/4816, so that one is now resolved.

Batched chat completions is a deeper issue though, since the Omni version of it apparently is not implemented yet 😅 for now, I've just added it to the rejection list for all models, so all models will give 400 + `Batched chat completions are not yet supported by vLLM Omni.` as a user facing message.

<details>
<summary>Updated output after rebasing on latest `main`</summary>

```
Server:  http://localhost:8998
Model:   Qwen/Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct
Voice:   aiden
Timeout: 120s (HTTP), 5s (WS)
──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

=== Info / Health ===

  GET /load                                           200  17B json
  GET /version                                        200  20B json
  GET /metrics                                        200  159028B text/plain

reviews.author: yuanheng-zhao
reviews.body:
Generally LGTM. 

One discussion left: For now do we expose server-side errors to request senders as well (like `500  'State' object has no attribute 'serving_tokenization'`)? If so, I think we still need to refine a bit
